mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the threshold, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or out-of-sample R-squared drops below 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity and corporate output growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during periods of capacity utilization above 85% or when inventory-to-sales ratios exceed 1.3.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid acceleration in consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in >70% of high-inflation months, or the signal shows no predictive accuracy after controlling for rate expectations in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of observations or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over rolling 5-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue streams, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are uncorrelated with GDP growth revisions, or the signal produces negative alpha after adjusting for market beta and volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output and deteriorating corporate margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: The signal generates positive forward returns in >60% of triggered instances or shows no predictive accuracy in out-of-sample backtests across multiple market regimes.
